_description: Turkish electrical standards for appliance and electronics compatibility.

mains_power.plug_type_alt: Type C (Europlug) also fits Type F outlets
mains_power.notes: Turkey uses European continental standard. US/Japan devices (110V) require step-down transformer.

residential_main.notes: Main breaker (ana şalter) typically 32A or 40A for apartments. 63A for larger homes.

circuit_breakers.rcd_requirement: 30mA RCD (kaçak akım rölesi) required for wet areas (bathroom, kitchen). Mandatory in new construction since 2004.

electric_oven.notes: Built-in ovens over 3kW often need hardwire installation by electrician.

induction_cooktop.plug: Hardwired connection required for most models
induction_cooktop.notes: Highest power kitchen appliance. Verify existing wiring before purchase. Older apartments may need electrical upgrade.

electric_water_heater.notes: Must be on RCD-protected circuit. Termosifon very common in Turkish homes.

air_conditioner_split.plug: Schuko 16A for 9000-18000 BTU. Dedicated circuit for 24000+ BTU.
air_conditioner_split.notes: Inverter models draw less peak current. Installation includes electrical connection.

electric_dryer.notes: Heat pump dryers draw significantly less power.

us_devices_110v.solution: Step-down transformer (220V → 110V). Must be rated above device wattage.
us_devices_110v.common_issues: US hair dryers, kitchen appliances, some electronics. Check label for '100-240V' -- those are universal.

uk_devices_type_g.solution: Simple plug adapter (Type G → Type F). No transformer needed (230V ≈ 220V).
uk_devices_type_g.notes: UK devices work fine electrically. Only need adapter.

universal_voltage_devices.how_to_check: Look for '100-240V 50/60Hz' on device label or charger
universal_voltage_devices.solution: Only need plug adapter if not Schuko/Europlug

energy_labels.rescaling_note: New scale (A-G) rolled out 2021-2024. Old A+++ ≈ new C/D for many categories. Check label date.
